commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts` to strip extracted runtime context from returned `modelPrompt` and adds prepend/append hook regression tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +41. Total +50 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: Slack queues the event, reply assembly prepends it, and hook-mutated `modelPrompt` currently keeps that same prefix after runtime-context extraction. I did not run live Slack or tests in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Member-authored PRs stay open for maintainer review; there is no discrete mechanical blocker for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(agents): strip system-event prefix from modelPrompt when before_prompt_build hooks add context
target.body:
Fixes #95323

### What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where a Slack DM (or any channel message that drains a system event) would deliver the inbound label — e.g. `System: [2026-06-20 13:59:51] Slack DM from Alice` — to the model **twice** in the same turn: once inside the hidden runtime-context custom message (Message A) and again inline in the model-facing user turn (Message B). Both occurrences carry the same timestamp, confirming a single event rendered at two sites.

### Why This Change Was Made

When `before_prompt_build` hooks return `prependContext` or `appendContext`, `attempt.ts` sets `hasPromptBuildContext = true` and passes `promptForModelBeforeRuntimeContextSplit` (the full effective prompt after hooks) as the `modelPrompt` argument to `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts`. The function correctly extracts the system-event prefix into `runtimeContext` (delivered as Message A), but the `modelPromptText` it builds from `modelPrompt.text` still contains that prefix verbatim. Because `modelPromptText !== transcriptPrompt`, the function returned it as `promptSubmission.modelPrompt`, which `buildCurrentInboundPrompt` then wrapped with `currentInboundContext` to produce Message B — with the event prefix still present.

The fix strips `hiddenRuntimeContext` from `modelPromptText` before returning, using the existing `removeLastPromptOccurrence` helper. That helper targets the last occurrence, so hook content that coincidentally contains the same text is not mis-targeted. When the substring is absent the fallback is `modelPromptText` unchanged. The guard `hiddenRuntimeContext && modelPrompt` is falsy in every pre-existing code path (either `hiddenRuntimeContext` resolves to `""` when `modelPrompt.text === extracted.text`, or `modelPrompt` is not provided), so the branch is a no-op for all callers outside the new hook+system-event scenario.

### User Impact

The system-event label now appears exactly once per turn in the model's context — only in the hidden runtime-context message. The duplicate inline occurrence in the user turn is eliminated, reducing context inflation and removing the confusing repetition.

### Evidence

**New regression tests (`runtime-context-prompt.test.ts`):** two unit tests assert that `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts` strips the system-event prefix from `modelPrompt` when hooks inject `prependContext` or `appendContext`, and places it in `runtimeContext` only. Both tests fail on `main` and pass after the fix.

**No regression on existing 23 tests in `runtime-context-prompt.test.ts`.** Every existing test that passes `modelPrompt` resolves `hiddenRuntimeContext` to `""` or leaves `modelPrompt` undefined; the new stripping branch is a provably unreachable no-op for those cases.

**Test runs (all green):**

- `runtime-context-prompt.test.ts` — 23 tests
- `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts` — 85 tests
- `get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` — 82 tests

**Autoreview:** clean, 0 accepted/actionable findings, correctness 0.85.
